title: nono Capability Manifest
description: A fully-resolved, portable capability manifest describing what a nono sandbox enforces. Manifests are the machine-consumable contract for sandbox configuration — they contain no inheritance, no legacy aliases, and no composition. Profiles compile down to manifests. NOTE: additionalProperties is true during schema development to allow forward-compatible evolution without breaking existing consumers. Switch to false once the schema stabilizes (post-1.0).

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI for editor validation and autocompletion.

version.description: Schema version (semver). Consumers use this to determine which fields are available.

network.description: Network access policy including domain filtering, endpoint rules, and port allowlists.

credentials.description: Credential definitions for the reverse proxy. Each credential configures a service route with secret injection and optional L7 endpoint filtering.

rollback.description: Rollback snapshot configuration controlling which files are excluded from undo snapshots.

Filesystem.description: Filesystem capability grants and denials. All paths are unresolved strings — canonicalization happens at enforcement time to keep manifests portable across machines.

grants.description: Filesystem access grants. Each grant specifies a path, access mode, and whether it targets a file or directory.

deny.description: Filesystem paths to deny access to. Deny rules take precedence over grants on platforms that support them (macOS/Seatbelt). On Linux/Landlock, deny is expressed by omitting grants.

FsGrant.description: A filesystem access grant for a single path.

path.description: Filesystem path to grant access to. May use ~ for home directory. Not canonicalized — resolution happens at enforcement time.

type.description: Whether this grant targets a file or directory. Defaults to directory.

AccessMode.description: Filesystem access mode. 'read' grants read-only access, 'write' grants write-only access, 'readwrite' grants both.

FsEntryType.description: Whether a filesystem grant targets a single file or a directory (recursive).

FsDeny.description: A filesystem path to deny access to.

path.description: Filesystem path to deny access to.

Network.description: Network access policy. Controls outbound connectivity, domain filtering, L7 endpoint restrictions, and port allowlists.

mode.description: Network access mode. 'blocked' denies all network access. 'proxy' routes traffic through the nono proxy for domain/endpoint filtering and credential injection. 'unrestricted' allows all network access (default).

allow_domains.description: Domains allowed through the proxy. Only meaningful when mode is 'proxy'. Supports exact hostnames and wildcard suffixes (e.g., '.googleapis.com').

endpoints.description: L7 endpoint definitions with per-host method+path filtering. Only meaningful when mode is 'proxy'.

ports.description: TCP port allowlists for fine-grained network control.

dns.description: Whether DNS resolution is allowed. Defaults to true.

NetworkMode.description: Network access mode for the sandboxed process.

NetworkEndpoint.description: An L7 endpoint definition with host-level access and optional method+path filtering.

rules.description: L7 method+path filtering rules. When non-empty, only requests matching at least one rule are allowed (default-deny). When empty or absent, all paths on this host are permitted.

EndpointRule.description: HTTP method+path access rule for restricting which API endpoints are accessible.

method.description: HTTP method to match (e.g., 'GET', 'POST') or '*' for any method.

path.description: URL path glob pattern. Uses standard glob syntax: '*' matches one path segment, '**' matches zero or more segments.

connect.description: TCP ports allowed for outbound connections.

localhost.description: Localhost TCP ports allowed for bidirectional IPC (connect + bind).

Credential.description: A credential route definition for the reverse proxy. Defines how to route requests to an upstream service and inject credentials the sandboxed process never sees.

name.description: Service name for this credential (e.g., 'github', 'internal_api'). Used to reference this credential from endpoint rules.

upstream.description: Upstream URL to proxy requests to (e.g., 'https://api.github.com'). Must use HTTPS, or HTTP only for loopback addresses.

source.description: Credential source URI. Supported schemes: 'env://VAR_NAME' (environment variable), 'file:///path' (file contents), 'op://vault/item/field' (1Password), 'apple-password://server/account' (macOS Keychain), or a bare account name for the system keystore.

inject.description: How the credential is injected into outbound requests. Defaults to Bearer token in Authorization header.

env_var.description: SDK API key environment variable name for phantom token injection (e.g., 'OPENAI_API_KEY'). Required when source is a URI manager reference (op://, apple-password://, file://) because those URIs cannot be meaningfully uppercased into an environment variable name. When absent for keystore-backed credentials, derived from credential_key.to_uppercase().

endpoint_rules.description: L7 endpoint rules for this credential route. When non-empty, only requests matching at least one rule are forwarded (default-deny). When empty or absent, all endpoints are permitted.

CredentialInject.description: Configuration for how a credential is injected into proxied requests.

header.description: HTTP header name for injection. Only used when mode is 'header'.

format.description: Format string for the credential value, using {} as placeholder. Only used when mode is 'header'.

path_pattern.description: Pattern to match in incoming URL path, using {} as placeholder for the phantom token. Required when mode is 'url_path'.

path_replacement.description: Pattern for outgoing URL path, using {} as placeholder for the real credential. Defaults to path_pattern if not specified. Only used when mode is 'url_path'.

query_param_name.description: Name of the query parameter to inject the credential into. Required when mode is 'query_param'.

InjectMode.description: Credential injection mode for the reverse proxy.

allowed_commands.description: Deprecated in v0.33.0. Startup-only command allowlist override. Not enforced for child processes.

blocked_commands.description: Deprecated in v0.33.0. Startup-only command denylist extension. Not enforced for child processes.

ipc_mode.description: IPC mode controlling POSIX semaphore access. Defaults to 'shared_memory_only'.

exec_strategy.description: Execution strategy for the sandboxed process. Defaults to 'monitor'.

SignalMode.description: Signal isolation mode. 'isolated' restricts signals to the current process only. 'allow_same_sandbox' allows signaling same-sandbox children. 'allow_all' allows signaling any process.

ProcessInfoMode.description: Process inspection mode. Controls whether the sandboxed process can read process info for other processes.

IpcMode.description: IPC mode. 'shared_memory_only' allows POSIX shared memory but denies semaphores. 'full' enables both (required for Python multiprocessing and similar runtimes).

ExecStrategy.description: Execution strategy. 'direct' execs into the command (nono disappears). 'monitor' sandboxes then forks (default). 'supervised' forks then sandboxes (supports rollback and capability elevation).

enabled.description: Whether rollback snapshots are enabled. Defaults to false.

exclude_patterns.description: Patterns to exclude from rollback snapshots. Matched against path components (exact match) or as substrings of the full path if they contain a slash.

exclude_globs.description: Glob patterns to exclude from rollback snapshots.
